messages.content:
Wharf paperwork run. Tool signatures (call -> result type):
- cranelift(scrip) -> docket
- gauging(cargo) -> permit
- berthing(cargo) -> crestmark
- lading(cargo, scrip) -> tallycard
- weighdeck(scrip, crestmark) -> sealmark
- sealing(cargo) -> quaytoken
- quayage(quaytoken, permit) -> scrip

Goal: obtain a sealmark for the gladeamber cargo.
Each correct call returns an opaque handle token of its result type;
later calls take those tokens as arguments. Where a signature says
cargo, pass the literal cargo name.

Calls so far, with returned handles:
1. berthing(gladeamber) -> crestmark CR-N4VR
2. gauging(gladeamber) -> permit PE-85GF
3. sealing(gladeamber) -> quaytoken QU-GG43

What is the next call toward the goal? Write it exactly as
name(arg) or name(arg1, arg2), using the literal cargo name or
handle tokens already returned.

End your reply with exactly one final line of the form:
ANSWER: <value>

Wharf paperwork run. Tool signatures (call -> result type):
- tariffing(docket, berthslip) -> crestmark
- weighdeck(chit, quaytoken) -> voucher
- pilotage(docket, berthslip) -> flagnote
- clerking(waxbadge) -> clearance
- cranelift(cargo) -> docket
- lading(berthslip) -> waxbadge
- gauging(voucher, clearance) -> sealmark
- sealing(cargo) -> berthslip
- hoisting(cargo) -> chit
- ropework(docket) -> quaytoken

Goal: obtain a sealmark for the gladeamber cargo.
Each correct call returns an opaque handle token of its result type;
later calls take those tokens as arguments. Where a signature says
cargo, pass the literal cargo name.

Calls so far, with returned handles:
1. sealing(gladeamber) -> berthslip BE-TRA7
2. lading(BE-TRA7) -> waxbadge WA-7DXU
3. clerking(WA-7DXU) -> clearance CL-RV39
4. cranelift(gladeamber) -> docket DO-E2HW
5. ropework(DO-E2HW) -> quaytoken QU-GB9E
6. hoisting(gladeamber) -> chit CH-9W5U

What is the next call toward the goal? Write it exactly as
name(arg) or name(arg1, arg2), using the literal cargo name or
handle tokens already returned.

End your reply with exactly one final line of the form:
ANSWER: <value>

You are a wharf paperwork runner. Call tools to produce the goal document.
Rules: each correct call returns '-> <type> <TOKEN>'; later calls consume tokens returned by earlier calls; where a signature says cargo, pass the literal cargo name. Bad calls waste the turn. The episode ends when the goal document is issued or turns run out.
Action grammar: CALL name(arg1) | CALL name(arg1, arg2)
Reply with exactly one action line and nothing else.

Tools:
- winching(chit) -> docket
- ropework(permit, quaytoken) -> warrant
- cranelift(cargo) -> permit
- sealing(cargo) -> chit
- lading(docket) -> quaytoken
- stowage(crestmark) -> clearance
- gauging(permit, waxbadge) -> signet
- weighdeck(sealmark, warrant) -> crestmark
- berthing(cargo) -> waxbadge
- clerking(waxbadge) -> sealmark
Goal: obtain a crestmark for the gladeamber cargo. Turn budget: 18.

You are a wharf paperwork runner. Call tools to produce the goal document.
Rules: each correct call returns '-> <type> <TOKEN>'; later calls consume tokens returned by earlier calls; where a signature says cargo, pass the literal cargo name. Bad calls waste the turn. The episode ends when the goal document is issued or turns run out.
Action grammar: CALL name(arg1) | CALL name(arg1, arg2)
Reply with exactly one action line and nothing else.

Tools:
- clerking(quaytoken) -> crestmark
- berthing(cargo) -> quaytoken
- cranelift(permit) -> berthslip
- lading(quaytoken) -> permit
- tariffing(permit, cargo) -> chit
Goal: obtain a berthslip for the gladeamber cargo. Turn budget: 6.

A sealed glyph machine transforms any string of exactly 4 glyphs
by one fixed hidden rule.
The glyph alphabet, in cycle order: za, ke, ro, mu, ti, fen, gor, lus (after lus the cycle wraps back to za).
The hidden rule is ONE of: (a) the whole string is reversed; (b) the glyphs at two secret fixed positions trade places; (c) one secret fixed position is overwritten with one secret fixed glyph; (d) every glyph steps forward the same secret count along the alphabet cycle.

Probe log (input -> output):
  lus-ti-ke-za -> mu-za-fen-ti
  za-ke-ke-ro -> ti-fen-fen-gor
  fen-ti-mu-mu -> ke-za-lus-lus

The machine now reads: za-fen-lus-gor
What does it print?
Write glyph strings dash-joined, e.g. za-ke-ro.

End your reply with exactly one final line of the form:
ANSWER: <value>

Bell-foundry commission ledger, in clerk order (not day order).
Line format: C<id> d<day>: <patron as written> b<bells> f<fee in marks>.
Amend d<n>: C<id> f=<marks> or b=<bells> corrects that field; the latest day wins.
Names not listed in a Note belong to different patrons.
Note: Sorvin Guild also signs as 'Sorvin & Kin' or 'Sorvins'.

C98 d4: Ostren Guild b9 f140
C90 d19: Ostren Guild b2 f162
C19 d39: Sorvind Order b2 f149
C84 d11: Ostren Guild b7 f85
C65 d41: Sorvin & Kin b5 f137
C68 d37: Ostren Guild b5 f35
Amend d74: C39 f=86
C37 d20: Halvet Lodge b2 f87
C36 d69: Quarel House b1 f29
C55 d51: Halvet Lodge b3 f125
Amend d91: C90 b=1
C77 d17: Sorvin Guild b9 f46
Amend d98: C19 b=1
C39 d29: Sorvins b8 f112

After all amendments, what is the total fee in marks across all commissions of Sorvin Guild?

End your reply with exactly one final line of the form:
ANSWER: <value>

A scout kept a log while exploring an underground burrow network of
chambers joined by corridors. Corridors run both ways: the return
direction is always the exact reverse (north-south, east-west, up-down).

Exploration log:
1. From Duskhollow we went east into Mosshollow.
2. From Mosshollow we went west into Duskhollow.
3. From Duskhollow we went north into Emberdeep.
4. From Emberdeep we went north into Gnarlden.

How many distinct chambers are named in the log?

End your reply with exactly one final line of the form:
ANSWER: <value>

The Tarnmoot fair is granting market stalls. Each stall pays a fee
(in gilds) and needs footage (in spans). Grant a subset of stalls so
the total fee is as large as possible while every rule holds.

Stalls:
S1 Pinchwick (herb): fee 58, footage 5
S2 Bramblewick (spice): fee 12, footage 7
S3 Snugharbor (spice): fee 13, footage 9
S4 Gorsequill (herb): fee 42, footage 8
S5 Nettlecombe (spice): fee 51, footage 7
S6 Mossgable (toys): fee 19, footage 3
S7 Hobbleworth (herb): fee 55, footage 7
S8 Thistledown (herb): fee 12, footage 6

Rules:
- Total footage of granted stalls must not exceed 42 spans.

Reply with the ids of the granted stalls, comma-separated, any order.

End your reply with exactly one final line of the form:
ANSWER: <value>
